page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-48 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The instance features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is configured with 96 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in compute-intensive tasks. The instance achieved a Geekbench multi-core score of 17695.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 54727.59, both placing in the strong category. It demonstrates top-tier performance in image processing with a score of 18505.0 and text processing with a score of 2733.0. For cryptographic and compression tasks, it registers a Passmark CPU encryption score of 43640.5 and a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 31.98 GB/sec. Large-block uncached memory read bandwidth is measured at 150.28 GB/sec, while write bandwidth is 64.01 GB/sec. Large language model inference speeds are also notable, processing 205.4 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a medium 7B model and 28.83 tokens/sec for text generation.

From a resource density perspective, the c4-highcpu-48 offers high compute capacity relative to its memory footprint. The 2.0 GB RAM per core ratio represents a tradeoff, making it less optimal for memory-bound applications but highly cost-efficient for raw CPU-bound processing. The lack of local storage requires external network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads. This instance is optimized for workloads such as software compilation, image and text processing, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-48 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 96 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 24 physical cores with hyper-threading and a 260 MB L3 cache. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, presenting a resource tradeoff of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core capabilities, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 17695.0 and top-tier performance in image and text processing. It also delivers strong results in LLM inference, processing 205.4 tokens/sec on a medium 7B model. This profile makes the instance highly efficient for compute-heavy tasks but less suitable for memory-intensive applications. It is designed for workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-48 is a compute-optimized instance with 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong multi-core and image processing performance.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 instance featuring forty-eight dedicated vCPUs, designed for high-throughput processing and CPU-bound workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor
96 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core
Strong multi-core performance with a Geekbench score of 17695.0
Top-tier benchmark results in image and text processing
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based inference
